title: Amauropelta longicaulis
taxonomy: Amauropelta longicaulis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. This genus is a part of the Thelypteridaceae family and is native to South America. Amauropelta longicaulis further belongs to the family of vascular plants known as Pteridophyta. It also falls under the division Magnoliophyta and the kingdom Plantae. Additionally, it is classified as an evergreen fern, with its specific classification being: Amauropelta longicaulis (Mett.) Pichi-Serm., which is a scientific name.

intro: Amauropelta longicaulis, also known as Chilean fern, is a type of fern native to South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress.
